Code evidence: src/app/api/health/route.ts; src/app/api/ready/route.ts; src/app/api/_lib/recursivConfig.ts; scripts/smoke-iqwars-prod.mjs; package.json smoke:prod
Verification evidence: pnpm smoke:prod passed 29 production checks against https://iqwars.app with redis-url storage and Recursiv project access; pnpm audit:source passed 185 checks including production smoke assertions; pnpm build passed; pnpm audit:launch passed 268 checks with live health/ready readiness proof
Next action: Keep smoke:prod in deploy/runbook checks and expand route snippets when adding launch-critical surfaces.

User story: As the operator, I want APIs to reject bad payloads, future/stale dates, duplicate locks, spoofed profiles, anonymous protected writes, and repeated abuse loops during public traffic spikes.
Expected behavior: APIs sanitize input, reject impossible scores, verify player keys where required, serialize updates, and enforce durable rate-limit buckets on public write-heavy surfaces before expensive provider calls or persistent side effects.
Code evidence: src/app/api/* routes; src/app/api/_lib/rateLimit.ts; scripts/audit-iqwars.mjs
Verification evidence: pnpm audit:source passed 165 checks, 0 failures; pnpm build passed; Coolify deployed commit aa8b55e162c3dec82cb70c57d6a6502fe989e0fc; pnpm audit:launch passed 247 checks, 0 failures, including durable Redis readiness, malformed scoring fuzz cases, duplicate/race-safe attempts and username claims, anonymous protected-write rejections, route-specific rate-limit source coverage for auth/reminders/checkout/gameplay/social writes, and a live username 429 proof after repeated claim attempts.
Next action: Keep live 429 proof and route-specific source checks in launch audit; tune limits only from real traffic data.

User story: As a visitor, I should not see a white unstyled flash before the dark app loads.
Expected behavior: layout includes critical CSS and app shell styling to reduce FOUC.

Verification evidence: pnpm audit:visual passed production dark html/body FOUC proxy on home, room, and rankings across mobile/desktop on 2026-06-28

User story: As the operator, I want pushed changes to reach iqwars.app reliably through Coolify.
Expected behavior: GitHub main deploys to Coolify app recursiv-iq-standalone serving iqwars.app; manual Coolify force-deploy path can push the current main commit when the webhook is not visible.
Code evidence: Git history; Coolify app metadata; production health; scripts/audit-visual-ux.mjs; /private/tmp/deploy-iq-coolify-current.mjs
Verification evidence: Coolify app recursiv-iq-standalone force-deployed commit 5fcab32cfe83f9b9b769516293528bebb2bdc6bd on 2026-06-28, deployment finished, /api/ready returned launchReady true with redis-url storage and Recursiv project access, and production visual audit passed 708 checks against https://iqwars.app after deploy; an earlier post-push production visual run still showed the old result-flow behavior, so automatic webhook visibility is not proven.
Issues: Auto-deploy webhook/path is not proven end-to-end, Coolify app metadata still lacks an explicit public commit endpoint/header, and the deploy path still lacks a proven no-downtime health-check/rolling deploy gate.
Next action: Add explicit commit endpoint/header, verify GitHub push webhook triggers Coolify without manual force, and enable a no-downtime health-check/rolling deploy gate before A+.
